mnemonics: P.I.E.S. for Resource Types: Protocols, Implementations, Examples, Standards. Think of the slices of a resource pie you need to consume to learn effectively.
keyPoints: Focus on official documentation and standards (e.g., NIST, ISO) for foundational knowledge.
Use practical implementation guides and code examples to understand application.
Supplement with case studies of real-world breaches to understand consequences and countermeasures.
commonMistakes: Relying solely on one type of resource, like only reading theory or only watching tutorial videos.
Using outdated or non-authoritative resources that may teach incorrect or deprecated practices.
Not connecting the resource material back to the core principles of data security (CIA triad) and privacy laws (like GDPR).
